name: Documentation Update

tasks.title: Update how-it-works.md
tasks.body: Update docs/humans/how-it-works.md to reflect that the daemon commits after each task iteration, not the agent. Remove any references to the agent running git commands. Describe the new flow: agent works on task, emits terminal marker, daemon processes marker, updates state, commits all changes (code + state) deterministically. Mention that commit behavior is configurable via git config fields.

tasks.title: Update configuration.md with new git config fields
tasks.description: Update configuration.md with new git config fields
tasks.body: Update docs/humans/configuration.md to document the full git config section: auto_commit (master switch), commit_on_success, commit_on_failure, commit_state, skip_hooks. For each field, document: the default value, what it controls, and how it interacts with other fields (e.g., fine-grained fields are ignored when auto_commit is false). Include an example JSON snippet showing the full git config block.

tasks.title: Update collaboration.md and failure-and-recovery.md
tasks.body: In docs/humans/collaboration.md: update the git integration section to describe daemon-driven auto-commits, branch safety, and how staging area preservation works. In docs/humans/failure-and-recovery.md: update the partial work preservation section to describe commit-on-failure behavior, the new commit message format for failures ('wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt <N>)'), and how the commit_on_failure config flag controls this. Also check README.md for any references to how commits work and update if needed.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Documentation Update is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Grep all docs/humans/*.md and AGENTS.md for stale references to agent-driven git operations
